rationale: Authority data for W40K-0131..W40K-0140 — a 2023-2025 cluster spanning the 10th-edition launch (Leviathan box-novel) through the early-Arks-of-Omen / post-Arks-of-Omen / Sabbat-Worlds-revival window. Tight thematic clustering this batch: a **Cadian-legacy mini-arc** (Longshot's Darya Nevic on Attruso → Creed: Ashes of Cadia's Ursula-Creed-daughter-of-Ursarkar legacy-mantle → Fall of Cadia's Ursarkar-Creed-last-stand 13th-Black-Crusade-prequel-causally → Hell's Last's Minka-Lesk-Cadian-101st-post-Cadia-survivor-arc on jungle-world Alia) — four of ten entries are Cadian-Astra-Militarum-primary with deliberate cross-generation continuity, the densest Cadian-Imperial-Guard cluster in the authority layer to date. **Three first-authority-layer-as-primary-protagonist faction-debuts** (Tyranids-as-on-page-primary-antagonist with the Norn-Emissary-Harbinger on-page in Leviathan + Emperor's-Children-as-primary-protagonist-Heretic-Astartes-Legion in Fulgrim: The Perfect Son + Ethereal-Caste-led-Elemental-Council Tau-spy-thriller in Elemental Council), plus the **first Daemon-Primarch-Fulgrim-POV** (second authority-layer Daemon-Primarch-POV after ssot-w40k-013's Angron), the **first authority-layer Norn-Emissary-Harbinger-on-page-antagonist**, the **first authority-layer Imperium-Civil-War-Cadia-Fall structural-event-novel** (Fall of Cadia), the **first authority-layer Pylon-Network-anti-Warp-research-frame** (Genefather), the **first authority-layer Sabbat-Worlds-Aeronautica-Imperialis-revival** (Interceptor City — direct sequel to Double Eagle ssot-w40k-009 after 20-in-universe + 21-publication years), and the **first authority-layer Bree-Jagdea-return-from-Double-Eagle character continuity**. Two direct sequels to prior authority-layer entries: Above and Beyond (Denny Flowers, Lucille-von-Shard-book-2) follows Outgunned (ssot-w40k-013 W40K-0121), and Interceptor City (Dan Abnett) follows Double Eagle (ssot-w40k-009 W40K-0090) — cross-batch character-continuity now resolving for the first time across the authority layer. One direct sequel within Astartes-tech-mystery: Genefather (Guy Haley) follows Belisarius Cawl: The Great Work (ssot-w40k-011 W40K-0103). One Jude-Reid-double-entry (Creed: Ashes of Cadia + Fulgrim: The Perfect Son — same author, ~18-month publication-spread, two faction-debut books). **Female-primary-POV density**: five-of-ten entries are female-primary-POV (Darya Nevic + Ursula Creed + Lucille von Shard returning from Outgunned + Bree Jagdea returning from Double Eagle + Minka Lesk), matching ssot-w40k-011's previous high-water-mark; if Elemental Council's ensemble-with-Ethereal-Yor'i-frame is counted as gender-mixed-but-female-anchor, this batch effectively pushes a Cadian-and-female-led 2023-2025 cluster. **Five `data_conflict` flags for missing author fields** in the roster (W40K-0136/0137/0138/0139/0140) — confirmed by WebSearch + Black-Library + Lexicanum + Goodreads + Simon-&-Schuster + Warhammer-Community + author-Rapid-Fire-interviews: Above and Beyond = Denny Flowers; Elemental Council = Noah Van Nguyen; Interceptor City = Dan Abnett; Hell's Last = Justin D Hill; Fulgrim: The Perfect Son = Jude Reid. Roster-mistag-cleanup deferred to Maintainer Excel-SSOT workflow per Brief-061 convention; books stay in this batch to keep the slot covered. **No `low_confidence` flags this iteration** — 2023-2025 is the publication-frontier zone for my training data (cutoff January 2026 covers all five 2025 releases plus their pre-order coverage cycle), but WebSearch + Black-Library + Lexicanum + The-Unseen-Library + At-Boundary's-Edge + Goonhammer + Track-of-Words + Warhammer-Community + author-Rapid-Fire-interviews + Simon-&-Schuster product pages all gave strong coverage on every entry. **WebSearch:** mean=1.0/book, max=1 (10 total searches — one obligatory plot-context search per book, no follow-up searches needed). **Resolver-Pre-Note**: cumulativeBefore=130 → cumulativeAfter=140; next natural resolver-pause-window is at cumulative=150 (`ssot-w40k-015`, W40K-0141..W40K-0150 — one iteration from this one). The surface-form-resolver-pile from the post-069 second-window (ssot-w40k-006..014 = ~90 Bücher) continues to grow, with the Cadian-legacy-mini-arc this batch consolidating Cadia / Cadian-Shock-Troops / Ursarkar-E.-Creed / 13th-Black-Crusade / Eye-of-Terror / Indomitus-Crusade canonical-resolver-targets, and the cross-batch character-continuity (Lucille-von-Shard / Bree-Jagdea / Belisarius-Cawl / Fabius-Bile) signalling that Authority-Layer-internal-resolver-work is now load-bearing alongside surface-form-canonical-resolution.

overrides.synopsis: On Regium, Lieutenant Castamon of the Ultramarines sees the first signs of a Tyranid invasion before the world itself is ready to believe it. His strike force tries to kill the xenos in the outer system, but Hive Fleet Leviathan answers with stranger beasts, spores that smother cities, and a Genestealer Cult uprising on Ascension Day. Apothecary Biologis Vultis may hold the key to adapting faster than the swarm, while the Harbinger hunts him through the collapse. A 10th-edition launch war-story of biological escalation and planetary dread.

Sergeant Darya Nevic of the Cadian 217th is a sniper on the frozen manufactorum world Attruso, where the T'au invasion has made every kill into propaganda. She is not Cadian-born, and the regiment's true sons never let her forget it, yet Imperial officers want her turned into a symbol while the T'au promise a cleaner loyalty across the lines. As winter and casualties close in, Darya has to decide whether she can become the legend her soldiers need without surrendering the person beneath it. A line-soldier war story about identity, morale, and useful lies.

Ursula Creed has spent decades building a command career far from the shadow of her father, the lost Lord Castellan of Cadia. Then Guilliman sends her back to the shattered planet to recover Ursarkar Creed's hidden battle plans, and the new Lord Castellan finds poxwalker-haunted ruins, rival Imperial agendas, and a legacy that will not stay buried. Cadia is dead, but it still has teeth. A survival-horror edged Astra Militarum novel about inheritance, command, and the cost of carrying a name the Imperium refuses to forget.

Belisarius Cawl gathers the Imperium's great institutions on the artificial world Pontus Avernes to unveil his work on the Pylon network, a plan he believes could blunt the Great Rift and save a broken galaxy. The audience he wants is political; the guest he gets is Fabius Bile, who has his own designs on the data, the experiment, and the meaning of human perfection. Their duel becomes part scientific war, part ideological knife-fight, with Pontus Avernes as laboratory and the Imperium's representatives as audience, collateral, and prize. A Mechanicus thriller about genius, hubris, and two monsters who both think they are improving mankind.

Cadia has guarded the Eye of Terror for ten thousand years, and Ursarkar Creed knows the latest Chaos retreat is only the breath before the real blow. Abaddon the Despoiler returns with the 13th Black Crusade, driving the Black Legion and its allies into a siege that pulls Guardsmen, Navy pilots, Space Marines, Sisters of Battle, traitors, and doomed civilians into the same planetary death spiral. This is the Fall of Cadia as war epic: field command under impossible pressure, the pylons, the Blackstone Fortress, the birth-pain of the Great Rift, and the moment the Imperium learns that even its strongest gate can break while its soldiers still refuse to kneel.

Lucille von Shard is now an Imperial fighter ace and living propaganda icon; Kile Simlex, who helped make her famous, has been buried in anonymous scribe-work. A message that claims to come from Shard drags him to the rebel world Deighton, where he finds a pilot worn hollow by endless war and an enemy ace tearing her squadron apart. To survive, Simlex has to help her perform the hero role she no longer believes in, while deciding whether the legend he built is what saved her or what broke her. A sequel to Outgunned that turns aerial combat into a study of fame, burnout, and the machinery that consumes its own heroes.

overrides.synopsis: On Cao Quo, a newly annexed human world keeps resisting the T'au Empire with sabotage, insurgency, and stubborn memory. Ethereal Yor'i forms an Elemental Council to pacify the crisis: Fire Caste discipline, Water Caste intelligence, Air Caste speed, Earth Caste ingenuity, and the Ethereal certainty that all must serve the Greater Good. But the rebellion understands the Empire too well, and the council's own priorities start to fracture under pressure from human resistance and T'au politics alike. A T'au political spy-thriller about occupation, doctrine, caste friction, and how much compromise an empire can survive before its bright philosophy becomes rule by force.

overrides.synopsis: More than twenty years after Enothis, Commander Bree Jagdea returns to the air war of the Sabbat Worlds Crusade. Over the hive city Vesperus, she joins Circus 66 for interception runs that may decide whether the Imperium can break the Archenemy's hold on the skies. The dogfights are fast, technical, and personal, but the deeper pressure is time: veterans carrying old wars into new cockpits, younger pilots learning from people already half-consumed by attrition, and every victory arriving too late for someone. A darker return to the Double Eagle register, all speed, grief, and exhausted courage.

flags.reason: Roster authors[] empty; canonical author confirmed by Black Library + Lexicanum + The-Unseen-Library + Warhammer-Community

overrides.synopsis: Commissar Minka Lesk and the Cadian 101st have survived the death of Cadia and the brutal campaigns that followed, but the jungle world Alia may be where their luck finally rots away. In the Gallows cluster, a rising darkness threatens the sector, and Lesk's dwindling command is pushed into a war where the terrain, weather, supply chain, and enemy all seem designed to grind Cadian endurance into mud. A post-Cadia Astra Militarum story in the Catachan Devil and Krieg vein: command pressure, jungle horror, and soldiers still trying to prove their world did not die with them.

flags.reason: Roster authors[] empty; canonical author confirmed by Black Library + Lexicanum + Warhammer-Community + Wargame-Portal

overrides.synopsis: Fulgrim, Daemon Primarch of the Emperor's Children, challenges his scattered warriors to conquer the Imperial world Crucible and bring him the Black Templar defending it. What should be a perfect slaughter becomes a grinding siege, while the III Legion's champions scheme for the Phoenician's favor and chase their own private definitions of excellence. Fulgrim watches, tempts, and shapes them all, turning war into performance and ambition into a weapon. A Slaaneshi character study of perfection, treachery, and zealotry answering excess across the walls.
